items.q: What is characteristic of Baroque music?
items.correct: Continuous bass line, ornamentation, terraced dynamics, and counterpoint.
items.why: Students project later styles backwards. Baroque (1600-1750) used basso continuo, harpsichord/organ, and terraced (sudden) dynamics. Piano hadn't yet replaced harpsichord.

items.q: What does modulation mean in music?
items.correct: Changing from one key to another within a piece.
items.why: Students conflate modulation with any musical change. Specifically, modulation is a KEY CHANGE, usually established by a perfect cadence in the new key - a structural device.

items.q: What is the function of the bridge in a pop song?
items.correct: To provide contrast before the final chorus, often by changing melody, key, or texture.
items.misconception: It is the same as the chorus.
items.random_wrong: It is the very first section.
items.why: Students confuse bridge with chorus because both are 'big' sections. The bridge appears once (usually after the second chorus) to provide CONTRAST and renew interest before returning.
It's 1685-1750. “1678-1741” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Vivaldi's Four Seasons was published in:
items.why: It's 1725. “1715” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Bach lived 1685 to 1750. What was his age at death (in years)?
items.why: Exact answer: 65. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
